jokes.text: Oh! Hi, kids! I'm an unlicensed hologram of LeVar Burton.
jokes.setup: Parody of Reading Rainbow's wholesome educational host
jokes.punchline: The qualifier 'unlicensed' immediately undermines any authority
jokes.explanation: Strong opener that establishes the parody tone with a clever legal disclaimer twist

jokes.text: So, throw another book on the fire and settle in...
jokes.setup: Expected promotion of reading and literacy
jokes.punchline: Casually suggests burning books instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion of the educational format with dark anti-literacy humor

jokes.text: Don't look for clues! Look for the damn thimble!
jokes.setup: Young detective eagerly offering to help find a thimble
jokes.punchline: Elderly person's blunt, profanity-laced frustration
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing generational clash and impatience

jokes.text: It's daytime, you big stupid.
jokes.setup: Detective using flashlight to look for clues
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact pointing out the obvious with mild insult
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective character consistency with economic delivery

jokes.text: You know she's got severe dementia.
jokes.setup: Granny acting cranky and confused
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of serious medical condition
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that explains character behavior but feels somewhat cheap

jokes.text: You never had a chair.
jokes.setup: Dad complaining his chair is gone
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact correction that he never had one
jokes.explanation: Absurdist logic delivered with perfect deadpan timing

jokes.text: I carefully hid it along with my emergency vodka.
jokes.setup: Dad explaining he didn't lose the deed
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of hidden alcohol stash
jokes.explanation: Good character detail that reveals alcoholism through casual mention

jokes.text: What time is it now? / I don't know! The clock was stolen!
jokes.setup: Lancy asking what time it is to establish deadline urgency
jokes.punchline: Dad's frustrated realization that the stolen clock held the time
jokes.explanation: Clean setup/punchline with perfect logical irony - the thing they need to track time is the stolen item

jokes.text: Why can't you just call me your friend Andi?
jokes.setup: Lancy introducing 'tomboyish friend Andi'
jokes.punchline: Andi's reasonable objection to the qualifier
jokes.explanation: Smart commentary on unnecessary gendered qualifiers that will be a running gag

jokes.text: It's just called the baseball team! / Because you're on it. / Before that, it was called the boys' baseball team.
jokes.setup: Andi correcting the team name
jokes.punchline: Lancy's innocent but problematic logic about why it changed
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that shows Lancy's obliviousness to the implications

jokes.text: So she's the boy who took my spot.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Andi being on the team
jokes.punchline: Boy's confused gender logic while being resentful
jokes.explanation: Continues the gender confusion theme with petty resentment

jokes.text: Why don't you just say I've got a great curveball? / I don't know. 'Cause I'm insecure?
jokes.setup: Boy qualifying his compliment about Andi's curveball
jokes.punchline: Surprisingly honest self-awareness about his insecurity
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment with unexpected emotional honesty that's both funny and touching

jokes.text: I'll handle this immediate area right around here.
jokes.setup: Andi volunteering to search the scary abandoned cave
jokes.punchline: Boy's obvious cowardice disguised as strategic planning
jokes.explanation: Standard cowardice joke but well-executed with character consistency

jokes.text: The Smardy Boys! / The Smardy Boys!
jokes.setup: Introduction of new characters as Hardy Boys parody
jokes.punchline: Name is clearly 'Smart-y Boys' but sounds ridiculous
jokes.explanation: Decent parody name that sounds appropriately silly for the format

jokes.text: Why can't you just call me your pal Chud? You somehow feel obligated to comment on my weight?
jokes.setup: Being called 'portly pal Chud'
jokes.punchline: Same complaint as Andi but about weight instead of gender
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Andi's complaint, extending the pattern to other characteristics

jokes.text: I call him Chubbo.
jokes.setup: Chud's complaint about weight comments
jokes.punchline: Another character immediately making it worse with cruder nickname
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that makes the situation worse right after the complaint

jokes.text: Why?!
jokes.setup: Being introduced as 'tomboyish friend' again
jokes.punchline: Andi's exasperated single-word reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - single word captures complete frustration

jokes.text: Step on it, Chubbo! I mean, if your jalopy can even go uphill with you in it.
jokes.setup: Urgency to drive somewhere quickly
jokes.punchline: Cruel weight joke disguised as concern about car performance
jokes.explanation: Continues the cruel treatment of Chud but feels mean-spirited

jokes.text: Ah! That's it! I can't go on living. I'm driving off the cliff!
jokes.setup: Being repeatedly insulted about his weight
jokes.punchline: Sudden dramatic suicidal declaration
jokes.explanation: Good escalation to absurdist extreme, makes the bullying consequences comic

jokes.text: And... here we are at Lancy's house! I must've made a wrong turn at the cliff.
jokes.setup: Dramatic suicide attempt by driving off cliff
jokes.punchline: Casual explanation that he simply missed the cliff
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist payoff that deflates the drama while being logically impossible

jokes.text: Chud, did you eat Andi's mitt? / Yes, but not the other stuff!
jokes.setup: Various items have gone missing
jokes.punchline: Chud admits to eating a baseball mitt but denies other thefts
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd character moment - admits to impossible act while maintaining honesty about limits

jokes.text: Shploikees! The whole house is gone!
jokes.setup: Individual items going missing from inside house
jokes.punchline: Realization that the entire house has vanished
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that builds the mystery to absurd proportions

jokes.text: Why can't you just call me the inventor?
jokes.setup: Being introduced as 'Tom Snift, the boy inventor'
jokes.punchline: Same complaint as others about unnecessary qualifiers
jokes.explanation: Continues the running gag but starting to feel repetitive

jokes.text: A little sound can get out, but that's it.
jokes.setup: Explaining that nothing can escape a black hole, not even light
jokes.punchline: Casual exception that undermines the scientific explanation
jokes.explanation: Good scientific absurdity that makes the impossible conveniently possible

jokes.text: Hurry! We can still save my emergency vodka! And my daughter.
jokes.setup: People are trapped and need rescuing
jokes.punchline: Dad prioritizes alcohol over his daughter
jokes.explanation: Dark character consistency callback showing misplaced priorities

jokes.text: You can count on your bitter, degraded sidekicks.
jokes.setup: Being asked to help rescue the heroes
jokes.punchline: Accurate but depressing self-description
jokes.explanation: Good character awareness of their mistreatment and status

jokes.text: Enjoy the hole!
jokes.setup: Asked to pull the heroes out of the black hole
jokes.punchline: Vengeful abandonment instead of helping
jokes.explanation: Satisfying revenge moment with perfect economy and character payoff

jokes.text: Ah, oui! Alcoholism.
jokes.setup: Captain mentioning medicine for his ailment
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact identification of the 'ailment' as alcoholism
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with medical euphemism exposed

jokes.text: Or perhaps perpetrator. I could never get a straight answer.
jokes.setup: Describing love interest as victim of war crimes
jokes.punchline: Casual uncertainty about whether she was victim or perpetrator
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor that casually mentions major moral ambiguity in romance

jokes.text: Do you accept Belgian waffles?
jokes.setup: Trying to buy fruit in exotic market
jokes.punchline: Offering breakfast food as currency
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist moment showing Fry's confusion about foreign customs

jokes.text: They say it can crank anything!
jokes.setup: Describing the golden crank of Khartoum
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation of 'crank' as a mechanical action
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay that sets up the time-travel device

jokes.text: Also, how's our fuel supply? / Delicious.
jokes.setup: Checking fuel levels during flight
jokes.punchline: Bender has been drinking the fuel and comments on taste
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment combining his alcoholism with mechanical nature

jokes.text: I addressed it to the wrong professor.
jokes.setup: Romantic reunion after decades of correspondence
jokes.punchline: Entire relationship based on postal error
jokes.explanation: Excellent plot twist that completely undermines the romantic storyline

jokes.text: Ooh, I love it when you speak European.
jokes.setup: Warbler speaking with accent/foreign phrases
jokes.punchline: Geographic ignorance treating 'European' as a language
jokes.explanation: Good character ignorance joke about geography and languages

jokes.text: Zoidy, no! You stupid lemming!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg doing something presumably destructive
jokes.punchline: Professor's frustrated animal comparison
jokes.explanation: Standard Professor/Zoidberg dynamic with good animal insult

jokes.text: Where in butt's ass is my fiancé?
jokes.setup: Looking for missing Dr. Geology
jokes.punchline: Creative profanity construction
jokes.explanation: Mildly creative profanity but not particularly clever

jokes.text: You're a priceless fossil dating to geologic antiquity!
jokes.setup: Dr. Geology calling the bone specimen priceless and ancient
jokes.punchline: Professor turning the description into an insult
jokes.explanation: Clever insult that uses the scientific language against its speaker

jokes.text: I shall call it, 'the theory of Plate Tectonics!'
jokes.setup: Using time travel to observe continental drift
jokes.punchline: Professor claiming to discover already established scientific theory
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Professor's ego and scientific credit-grabbing

jokes.text: Uh, which one are you again? / I don't remember. / Eh, whatever. Who cares?
jokes.setup: Romantic reconciliation after solving the mystery
jokes.punchline: Complete indifference to identity after all the drama
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of romantic storyline with casual indifference

jokes.text: Oh, wait. I read them out of order. Anyway...
jokes.setup: Claiming to have saved the best story for last
jokes.punchline: Admission of reading stories randomly, undermining the curation
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about the show's structure and host competence

jokes.text: Here's your bike back! / That solves the mystery of the missing bike!
jokes.setup: Wikipedia mentioning someone stole his bike
jokes.punchline: Immediate return and case closure in seconds
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist pacing that parodies detective story structure

jokes.text: That's me, lady. Gimme 25 cents!
jokes.setup: Being asked to solve a mystery
jokes.punchline: Immediately demanding payment upfront
jokes.explanation: Good parody of Encyclopedia Brown's payment system

jokes.text: And I'm his bodyguard Lolly. I like to punch people.
jokes.setup: Introduction as bodyguard
jokes.punchline: Simple, direct statement of violent preference
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment with economic violence preference

jokes.text: O-On the surface of the sun! W-With my girlfriend!
jokes.setup: Claws trying to provide an alibi
jokes.punchline: Increasingly impossible claims under pressure
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of impossible alibi claims

jokes.text: The girlfriend part doesn't ring true! Arrest him, Lolly!
jokes.setup: Claws providing increasingly absurd alibi details
jokes.punchline: Wikipedia finds only the girlfriend claim unbelievable
jokes.explanation: Excellent joke structure - accepts impossible physics but not social scenario

jokes.text: Two murderers and one unlicensed hotdog vendor.
jokes.setup: Describing sending three criminals to electric chair
jokes.punchline: Mixing capital crimes with minor licensing violation
jokes.explanation: Good juxtaposition of serious and trivial crimes with same punishment

jokes.text: In your garage, talkin' to you, you id-jit!
jokes.setup: Wikipedia asking where someone was at specific time
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the obvious with mild insult
jokes.explanation: Standard pointing-out-the-obvious joke with character flavor

jokes.text: loiterin' in an alley with the other local toughs, smokin' a lollipop.
jokes.setup: Describing criminal activity location
jokes.punchline: Innocent candy substitute for cigarettes
jokes.explanation: Good juxtaposition of tough guy image with innocent behavior

jokes.text: He went to the chair for that taffy caper. / That's the way to go. Sittin' down.
jokes.setup: Claws Pinchy was executed for stealing taffy
jokes.punchline: Positive spin on electric chair execution method
jokes.explanation: Dark humor callback about disproportionate punishment with comfort focus

jokes.text: Hell no! I don't know you! I'm not lendin' you my magnifying glass!
jokes.setup: Neil deGrasse Tyson asking to borrow magnifying glass
jokes.punchline: Kid's emphatic refusal to help famous scientist
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing kid's stubbornness even with celebrity scientist

jokes.text: So, that's where it came from. The Big Bang.
jokes.setup: Complex mystery about flaming dog poop origin
jokes.punchline: Technically correct but useless cosmic origin explanation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion using scientific accuracy to provide completely unhelpful mystery solution

jokes.text: Of course! It was so obvious!
jokes.setup: Neil's Big Bang explanation for dog poop origin
jokes.punchline: Wikipedia's enthusiastic acceptance of non-solution
jokes.explanation: Good reaction that extends the absurdity by accepting the non-answer

jokes.text: It was the Big Bang guy! Get him!
jokes.setup: Neil explaining his presence at the Big Bang
jokes.punchline: Mob justice for cosmic event participation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that treats cosmic event as prosecutable crime

jokes.text: You got a place I can hide where no one ever goes? / Sure! Right over here.
jokes.setup: Neil asking for somewhere no one goes
jokes.punchline: LeVar pointing to the library/reading area
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-commentary on literacy decline and library usage

jokes.text: And remember, in space, no one can hear you read.
jokes.setup: Educational closing message about reading
jokes.punchline: Parody of Alien movie tagline applied to literacy
jokes.explanation: Perfect closing joke combining horror movie reference with reading promotion
